title: GitHubAppCredentialValue
description: Producer/consumer contract for the JSON value sealed inside a `github-app` credential. Producer = generacy-cloud (cluster activation + credential refresh). Consumer = packages/control-plane/src/services/wizard-env-writer.ts (mapCredentialToEnvEntries). This schema documents the field shape and which fields the consumer reads.

token.description: Refreshed GitHub App installation token. Consumed as GH_TOKEN. If missing or empty, the consumer emits no entries for this credential.

installationId.description: GitHub App installation ID. Producer-only; not read by consumer.

expiresAt.description: ISO 8601 token expiry. Producer-only; cloud handles refresh.

accountLogin.description: GitHub login of the installation account — org name for org installs, user login for user installs. Consumed as the fallback source for GH_USERNAME / GH_EMAIL when `gitIdentityLogin` is missing, empty, whitespace-only, or non-string. Trimmed before length check. Must remain emitted indefinitely (used by producer for non-identity purposes).

gitIdentityLogin.description: NEW in this feature. The operator-selected 'acting account' from the cloud activation wizard's 'Act as' picker (generacy-cloud#812). When present and non-empty after `.trim()`, the consumer uses this as GH_USERNAME and as the local-part of GH_EMAIL (`<login>@users.noreply.github.com`). Absent on credentials sealed before #812 — back-compat fallback to `accountLogin` is required.

$comment: Consumer reads only `token`, `accountLogin`, `gitIdentityLogin`. Other fields (installationId, expiresAt, anything additionalProperties) are passed through unchanged. `additionalProperties: true` reflects the producer's `{ ...installationData, token, expiresAt, ... }` flat-spread shape.
